task_completion.description: Open the payroll_engine project in PyCharm
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyCharm IDE is open with the directory '/home/ga/PycharmProjects/payroll_engine' visible in the Project explorer panel.

task_completion.description: Fix financial drift by using precise decimal calculations
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows the use of Python's 'decimal' library (e.g., 'from decimal import Decimal', 'Decimal()') for financial calculations instead of standard floating-point numbers.

task_completion.description: Implement progressive tax brackets instead of a flat top-bracket rate
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows logic that calculates tax progressively across multiple brackets (e.g., iterating through brackets, calculating tax on the portion of income within each bracket) rather than multiplying the entire income by a single rate.

task_completion.description: Cap Social Security deductions at the 2024 wage base limit
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows a conditional check against the value '168600' (or '168,600') to halt Social Security tax deductions once the employee's wage exceeds this limit.

task_completion.description: Calculate overtime based on a 40-hour work week
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor shows overtime calculation logic explicitly checking for hours exceeding 40 per week (e.g., 'hours > 40', 'hours - 40') rather than aggregating over a longer pay period.

task_completion.description: Run and pass the required test suite
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyCharm test runner or terminal shows successful execution (passing status) of tests including 'test_currency_precision', 'test_tax_progressive_brackets', 'test_ss_wage_base_limit', and 'test_overtime_weekly_aggregation'.

integrity.description: Agent used the PyCharm IDE to edit code
integrity.visual_evidence: Code modifications are visible within the PyCharm editor interface, not just executed via terminal commands like 'sed' or 'echo'.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely ran the test suite
integrity.visual_evidence: Test results appear in the standard PyCharm Run/Debug tool window or a genuine terminal pytest/unittest output, rather than being manually printed or mocked.

integrity.description: Agent did not hardcode the test answers to bypass logic
integrity.visual_evidence: The code contains actual logic for decimals, progressive taxes, and overtime, rather than just returning hardcoded expected values specifically for the test cases.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is exactly '/home/ga/PycharmProjects/payroll_engine'. The agent must fix four specific bugs. Look for the usage of Python's 'decimal' library to fix financial drift. Look for the hardcoded limit of 168600 for the Social Security wage base limit. The test suite includes 'test_currency_precision', 'test_tax_progressive_brackets', 'test_ss_wage_base_limit', and 'test_overtime_weekly_aggregation'. A passing score requires these tests to pass.
